title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
35K Salary Term Insurance Plan
When planning for the future, one of the most affordable and effective ways to secure your family's financial well-being is through a term insurance plan. A ₹35,000 monthly salary might seem modest, but investing in term life insurance ensures that, in case of an untimely death, your loved ones are financially protected. This article will explore why a term insurance policy is essential, how it works, and how to select the right term plan that suits your needs.
Why is Investing a Portion of Your Salary in Term Insurance Plans the Right Way to Go?
If you're earning around ₹35K per month, a term insurance plan can be a smart, affordable way to secure your family’s future. Like all other term insurance plans, your term insurance plan purchased with ₹35,000 salary can protect your loved ones in case of your untimely demise.
Affordable financial protection: Term insurance offers high coverage at low premiums, even for people with a ₹35,000 monthly income.
Covers loans and expenses: It helps the nominee pay off debts, EMIs, and everyday household costs.
Peace of mind: Your family won’t have to compromise on their lifestyle even after your death.
Customization is possible: Riders like critical illness, accidental death benefits, etc can be added for wider protection.
How Does Term Insurance Work for Individuals Earning ₹35K?
The way a term plan  works is by offering life insurance for a set amount of time. The nominee gets a lump sum or periodic payment (depending on the option chosen) known as the sum assured if the life assured  dies within specified time frame i.e. the policy term . The amount obtained from the sum assured can be used to cover Living expenses, debts, and upcoming commitments etc.
Term insurance is especially advantageous for those earning ₹35,000 per month for the following reasons:
It offers high coverage at low premiums, making it an affordable option. [2]
The death benefit received to the life assured's family can help in handling debts, outstanding loans, and EMIs in the event of the  life assured's passing. [2]
Some term plans come with a “Return of Premium” (TROP) feature, which refunds premiums paid if the life assured survives the policy term, unlike standard term insurance plans that only provide life coverage. [2]
Key Benefits of Term Life Insurance for ₹35K Salary Earners
A term insurance plan can provide strong financial security, even if you have a modest income of 35k per month.
Financial SecurityThe peace of mind that comes from knowing your family is financially secure in your absence is priceless. A term insurance plan help in ensuring that your loved ones do not face a financial crisis if something happens to you. This is especially crucial for individuals with dependents such as children or elderly parents who rely on your income.
Tax BenefitsOne of the additional advantages of term life insurance  is the tax benefit. Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act (in case of old regime), the premiums paid for a term insurance plan are eligible for a deduction of up to ₹1.5 lakh annually. This can significantly reduce your taxable income, leaving you with additional savings that can be directed towards other financial goals.[1]
Affordable Premiums
Term insurance is often the most affordable form of life insurance available today. Some term plans cost less compared to other life insurance plans. Even if you earn ₹35,000 a month, you can secure a high cover without straining your budget or compromising on the many benefits of this type of insurance.
Flexibility with Riders
You can add riders like accidental death benefit, critical illness, etc coverage to add more value to your basic coverage. These provide extra protection at a small additional cost.
Return of Premium (TROP) Option
If you outlive the policy term, return of premium term plans refund the total premiums paid over the policy duration. This is ideal for those who prefer not to “lose” money on a pure protection plan.
What’s the Ideal Investment Amount for a ₹35,000 Monthly Income?
When buying a term insurance plan in India with 35k salary, aim for a sum assured that’s at least 10 times your annual income. For a salary of ₹35,000 a month, your cover should be at least ₹42 lakh.
You can also calculate your personalized term cover using methods like the Income Replacement Method, Human Life Value (HLV), or Expense Replacement Method. These calculations will help you get a term plan that matches your family’s financial needs.
How Much Coverage Should You Opt for in a Term Plan with ₹35K Salary?
When considering a  term insurance plan, it’s important to choose an appropriate coverage amount. A common recommendation is to select a sum assured that is 10–12 times your annual income.3 For example, if your monthly salary is ₹35,000, your annual income would be ₹4,20,000, and the ideal coverage amount would range between ₹42 lakh to ₹50 lakh.
However, coverage needs can vary depending on several factors such as:
Outstanding debts (e.g., home loan, car loan)
Children's educational expenses
Financial Goals
Retirement needs
Choosing an accurate coverage amount that meets your family’s future needs is essential.
How to Choose the Right Term Plan for a ₹35K Monthly Salary
Choosing the right term insurance plan in India with a 35k salary means looking beyond just low premiums. The plan should offer enough coverage for your family’s needs, fit comfortably into your budget, and come from an insurer with a strong track record.
Premiums:Ensure the premiums are within your budget. Even though term insurance is relatively inexpensive, it’s essential to choose a policy that aligns with your financial situation.
Adequate Coverage:Ensure the policy offers sufficient coverage based on your income, dependents, and financial obligations. A coverage amount of 10–12 times your annual salary is typically recommended. [3]
Riders for Added Protection:Some term plans offer additional riders like critical illness coverage  or accidental death benefits at an additional nominal premium. These riders can help provide more comprehensive protection.
Claim Settlement Ratio:Look for an insurer with a high claim settlement ratio. The claim settlement ratio indicates the percentage of claims an insurer has successfully settled , therefore to  ensure that your nominees  receive the sum assured efficiently in case of a claim and hence, you should look for an insurer with a high claim settlement ratio. [6]
Conclusion
A term life insurance plan is one of the best ways to provide for your family’s financial security. With low premiums and extensive coverage, you can ensure that your loved ones are financially protected, even on a modest income. It’s essential to select the right plan that suits your needs and to carefully calculate the coverage required. Taking these steps today can help secure a brighter, more stable financial future for your family.
FAQs
What should be the ideal coverage amount if I earn ₹35,000 per month? The ideal coverage should be 10–12 times your annual income. For a ₹35,000 monthly salary, this would be ₹42 lakh to ₹50 lakh.[3]
Can I increase coverage for a ₹35K salary term insurance plan?Yes, coverage can be increased by choosing increasing cover, buying new policy or by adding riders such as critical illness coverage.
Can I buy term insurance with a 35k salary per month if I have pre-existing health conditions?Yes, but you would have to declare any pre-existing medical conditions. Depending on your health and other factors, insurers will fix the premium payable etc . [3]
Can I buy term insurance for my entire family if I earn 35k per month?Yes, you can buy individual term policies for family members based on your coverage needs & financial capacity.
What would the premium be for a term insurance plan with ₹35K salary?The premium will depend on your age, coverage, and policy term amongst other factors.
How does the claim settlement process work for term insurance plans?The claim settlement procedure entails knowing the type of claim, making sure that disclosures are clear, paying premiums on time, filing the claim as soon as possible, and supplying the required paperwork for validation. [4]
What is the best age to buy term insurance?It’s ideal to purchase term insurance at a young age to lock in lower premiums and gain longer coverage.
Are riders available with term insurance plans for ₹35K salary earners?Yes, various riders such as critical illness rider, accidental death benefit rider etc  can be added to your plan at an additional nominal premium for enhanced benefits. Can I get a refund of my premiums if I don’t claim in a term insurance plan?In a standard term plan, premiums are not refunded. However, there are plans with a Return of Premium option, where premiums are refunded at the end of the policy term subject to terms and conditions of the policy.[5]
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions, please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy
full_pdf_text:
35K Salary Term Insurance Plan
When planning for the future, one of the most affordable and effective ways to secure your family's
financial well-being is through a term insurance plan. A ₹35,000 monthly salary might seem modest, but
investing in term life insurance ensures that, in case of an untimely death, your loved ones are
financially protected. This article will explore why a term insurance policy is essential, how it works, and
how to select the right term plan that suits your needs.
Why is Investing a Portion of Your Salary in Term Insurance Plans is the Right Way to Go?
If you're earning around ₹35K per month, a term insurance plan can be a smart, affordable way to secure your family’s financial future in case of your untimely demise.
● Affordable financial protection: Term insurance offers sizeable coverage at low premiums, even for people with a ₹35,000 monthly income. ● Covers loans and expenses: The death payout can help the nominee pay off debts, EMIs, and everyday household costs incase of the untimely death of the life assured. ● Peace of mind: Your family won’t have to compromise on their lifestyle even after your death. ● Additional coverage through riders: Riders like critical illness benefit rider, accidental death benefit rider , etc can be added for enhanced financial protection. How Does Term Insurance Work for Individuals Earning ₹35K?
The way a term plan works is by offering life insurance for a set amount of time. The nominee gets a
lump sum or periodic payment (depending on the option chosen) known as the sum assured if the life
assured dies within specified time frame i.e. the policy term . The amount obtained from the sum
assured can be used to cover Living expenses, debts, and upcoming commitments etc.
Term insurance is especially advantageous for those earning ₹35,000 per month for the following
reasons:
● It offers high coverage at low premiums, making it an affordable option. [2]
● The death benefit received to the life assured's family can help in handling debts, outstanding
loans, and EMIs in the event of the life assured's passing. [2]
● Some term plans come with a “Return of Premium” (TROP) feature, which refunds premiums
paid if the life assured survives the policy term, unlike standard term insurance plans that only
provide life coverage. [2]
Key Benefits of Term Life Insurance for ₹35K Salary Earners
A term insurance plan can provide strong financial security, even if you have a modest income of 35k per month.
Financial Security
The peace of mind that comes from knowing your family is financially secure in your absence is priceless.
A term insurance plan help in ensuring that your loved ones do not face a financial crisis if something
happens to you. This is especially crucial for individuals with dependents such as children or elderly
parents who rely on your income.
Tax Benefits
One of the additional advantages of term life insurance is the tax benefit. Under Section 80C of the
Income Tax Act (in case of old regime), the premiums paid for a term insurance plan are eligible for a
deduction of up to ₹1.5 lakh annually. This can significantly reduce your taxable income, leaving you
with additional savings that can be directed towards other financial goals.[1]
Affordable Premiums
Term insurance is often the most affordable form of life insurance available today. Even if you earn ₹35,000 a month, you can secure a sizeable cover without straining your budget .
Additional coverage via riders
You can add riders like accidental death benefit rider, critical illness benefit rider, etc to enhance the coverage of your plan . These provide added protection at a small additional cost.
Return of Premium (ROP) Option
If you outlive the policy term, return of premium term plans refund the total premiums paid over the policy duration.
What’s a suitable Investment Amount for a ₹35,000 Monthly Income?
When buying a term insurance plan in India with 35k salary, aim for a sum assured that’s at least 10 times your annual income. For a salary of ₹35,000 a month, your cover should be at least ₹42 lakh.
You can also calculate your sum assured using methods like the Income Replacement Method, Human Life Value (HLV), or Expense Replacement Method. These calculations will help you get an idea of the sum assured that will be required to financially secure your loved ones. How Much Coverage Should You Opt for in a Term Plan with ₹35K Salary?
When considering a term insurance plan, it’s important to choose an appropriate coverage amount. A
common recommendation is to select a sum assured that is 10–12 times your annual income.3 For
example, if your monthly salary is ₹35,000, your annual income would be ₹4,20,000, and the ideal
coverage amount would range between ₹42 lakh to ₹50 lakh.
However, coverage needs can vary depending on several factors such as:
● Outstanding debts (e.g., home loan, car loan)
● Children's educational expenses
● Financial Goals
● Retirement needs
Choosing an accurate coverage amount that meets your family’s future needs is essential.
How to Choose the Right Term Plan for a ₹35K Monthly Salary
Choosing the right term insurance plan in India with a 35k salary means looking beyond just low premiums. The plan should offer enough coverage for your family’s needs, fit comfortably into your budget, and come from an insurer with a strong track record.
1. Premiums:
Ensure the premiums are within your budget. Even though term insurance is relatively
inexpensive, it’s essential to choose a policy that aligns with your financial situation.
2. Adequate Coverage:
Ensure the policy offers sufficient coverage based on your income, dependents, and financial
obligations. A coverage amount of 10–12 times your annual salary is typically recommended.
[3]
3. Riders for Added Protection:
Some term plans offer additional riders like critical illness coverage or accidental death
benefits at an additional nominal premium. These riders can help provide more
comprehensive protection.
4. Claim Settlement Ratio:
Look for an insurer with a high claim settlement ratio. The claim settlement ratio indicates
the percentage of claims an insurer has successfully settled , therefore to ensure that your
nominees receive the sum assured efficiently in case of a claim and hence, you should look
for an insurer with a high claim settlement ratio. [6]
Conclusion
A term life insurance plan is one of the best ways to provide for your family’s financial security. With low
premiums and extensive coverage, you can ensure that your loved ones are financially protected, even
on a modest income. It’s essential to select the right plan that suits your needs and to carefully calculate
the coverage required. Taking these steps today can help secure a brighter, more stable financial future
for your family.
FAQs
What should be the ideal coverage amount if I earn ₹35,000 per month?
The ideal coverage should be 10–12 times your annual income. For a ₹35,000 monthly salary, this would
be ₹42 lakh to ₹50 lakh.[3]
Can I increase coverage for a ₹35K salary term insurance plan?
Yes, coverage can be increased by choosing increasing cover, buying new policy or by adding riders such
as critical illness coverage.
Can I buy term insurance with a 35k salary per month if I have pre-existing health conditions?
Yes, but you would have to declare any pre-existing medical conditions. Depending on your health and
other factors, insurers will fix the premium payable etc . [3]
Can I buy term insurance for my entire family if I earn 35k per month?
Yes, you can buy individual term policies for family members based on your coverage needs & financial
capacity.
What would the premium be for a term insurance plan with ₹35K salary? The premium will depend on your age, coverage, and policy term amongst other factors. How does the claim settlement process work for term insurance plans? The claim settlement procedure entails knowing the type of claim, making sure that disclosures are clear, paying premiums on time, filing the claim as soon as possible, and supplying the required paperwork for validation. [4]
What is the best age to buy term insurance?
It’s ideal to purchase term insurance at a young age to lock in lower premiums and gain longer coverage.
Are riders available with term insurance plans for ₹35K salary earners?
Yes, various riders such as critical illness rider, accidental death benefit rider etc can be added to your
plan at an additional nominal premium for enhanced benefits. Can I get a refund of my premiums if I
don’t claim in a term insurance plan?
In a standard term plan, premiums are not refunded. However, there are plans with a Return of
Premium option, where premiums are refunded at the end of the policy term subject to terms and
conditions of the policy.[5]
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions, please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy BJAZ-WEB-EC-16453/25

changes.word_original: How does the claim settlement process work for term insurance plans?The claim settlement procedure entails knowing the type of claim, making sure that disclosures are clear, paying premiums on time, filing the claim as soon as possible, and supplying the required paperwork for validation. changes.pdf_final: Can I buy term insurance for my entire family if I earn 35k per month?

changes.word_original: However, there are plans with a Return of Premium option, where premiums are refunded at the end of the policy term subject to terms and conditions of the policy.[5]
changes.pdf_final: Yes, you can buy individual term policies for family members based on your coverage needs & financial
capacity.
What would the premium be for a term insurance plan with ₹35K salary?
The premium will depend on your age, coverage, and policy term amongst other factors.
How does the claim settlement process work for term insurance plans?

The claim settlement procedure entails knowing the type of claim, making sure that disclosures are clear, paying premiums on time, filing the claim as soon as possible, and supplying the required paperwork for validation. [4]
What is the best age to buy term insurance?
It’s ideal to purchase term insurance at a young age to lock in lower premiums and gain longer coverage.
Are riders available with term insurance plans for ₹35K salary earners?
Yes, various riders such as critical illness rider, accidental death benefit rider etc can be added to your
plan at an additional nominal premium for enhanced benefits.
Can I get a refund of my premiums if I
don’t claim in a term insurance plan?
In a standard term plan, premiums are not refunded.
However, there are plans with a Return of
Premium option, where premiums are refunded at the end of the policy term subject to terms and
conditions of the policy.[5]
